name: IT-Grundschutz
owner: Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik (BSI)
description: BSI's IT-Grundschutz — German standard methodology for information security management, with detailed protection-requirement modules. The methodology aligns with ISO/IEC 27001 with German-specific guidance.
relationToCloudSecurity: Cloud-component modules included in current IT-Grundschutz catalogs.
relationToAISecurity: Applies to AI systems within scope.

license.notes: Government publication. Verify per-jurisdiction reuse terms.

current_extraction.notes: BSI publishes the Compendium as a single 800-page PDF plus a separate XLSX of per-module cross-reference tables. The Compendium PDF OOM'd marker on the GPU box (88s before kill) — too large for a single pass. We use the XLSX cross-reference as the structured source instead: extract_it_grundschutz.py consolidates the 104 per-module CSVs (generated by excel_to_csv.py) into a master per-requirement table (1,682 requirements across 10 layers: APP, CON, DER, IND, INF, ISMS, NET, OPS, ORP, SYS). The Compendium PDF is retained for reference; its narrative content (module descriptions, threat descriptions, implementation guidance) is not currently structured-extracted.

notes: Metadata stub; acquire source and extract.
